title: Avoid the use of the root account
description: The AWS root account has unrestricted access to all resources; regular use significantly increases blast radius if credentials are compromised.

title: Ensure MFA is enabled for the root account
description: Root account MFA is a fundamental control against account takeover and unauthorized privileged access.

title: Ensure hardware MFA is enabled for the root account
description: Hardware-backed MFA on the root account reduces phishing and SIM-swap risks compared with weaker factors.

title: Ensure IAM password policy requires strong passwords
description: Weak account password policies increase risk of brute-force and credential-stuffing attacks against console users.

title: Ensure IAM password policy prevents password reuse
description: Password reuse allows previously exposed credentials to be used again and weakens account hygiene.

title: Ensure MFA is enabled for all IAM users with console password
description: Console users without MFA are vulnerable to credential theft and unauthorized sign-in.

title: Ensure no active access keys for the root account
description: Root access keys provide long-lived unrestricted API access and are a high-value target for attackers.

title: Ensure access keys are rotated every 90 days or less
description: Long-lived access keys increase exposure time if compromised and reduce credential lifecycle hygiene.

title: Ensure S3 Block Public Access is enabled at account level
description: Account-level Block Public Access prevents accidental public exposure of buckets and sensitive data leakage.

title: Ensure S3 buckets are not publicly accessible
description: Publicly accessible buckets can expose confidential data and are a common cause of major cloud breaches.

title: Ensure S3 bucket versioning is enabled
description: Versioning supports recovery from accidental deletion or malicious overwrites, improving resilience.

title: Ensure default encryption is enabled for S3 buckets
description: Encryption at rest for S3 protects data confidentiality and reduces impact of unauthorized storage access.

title: Ensure CloudTrail is enabled in all regions
description: Multi-region CloudTrail logging is required for complete audit visibility and effective incident response.

title: Ensure CloudTrail log file validation is enabled
description: Log file integrity validation helps detect tampering and preserves audit evidence trustworthiness.

title: Ensure CloudTrail logs are encrypted with KMS
description: Encrypting CloudTrail logs with KMS strengthens control over audit log confidentiality and key management.

title: Ensure CloudTrail is integrated with CloudWatch Logs
description: CloudTrail-to-CloudWatch integration enables near-real-time detection and alerting for suspicious activity.

title: Ensure no security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to port 22
description: Unrestricted SSH exposure to the internet significantly increases risk of brute-force and unauthorized remote access.

title: Ensure no security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to port 3389
description: Open RDP access from anywhere creates a high-risk attack surface for credential and endpoint compromise.
